lore_age: 18th-century Bangkok Ramakien recension, from older Ramayana and Ayutthaya material
dex_note: Feeds on bananas and seafoam snacks between quests.
origin_story: Macchanu is the monkey-merfolk son of Hanuman and the mermaid Suvannamaccha in the Thai Ramakien. Raised beneath the sea, he guards the underwater realm with a prince's pride and a trickster's confidence. When Hanuman meets him, their fight turns into recognition, linking Rama's land quest with the ocean world.

lore_age: Early 19th-century Rama II-era stage tradition, based on older Phichit folktale
dex_note: Looks fierce, but this version mostly hoards shiny lotus seeds.
origin_story: Chalawan is the magical crocodile lord of Phichit's waters. He lives in a jewel-lit underwater cave and abducts Taphao Thong, pulling the hero Krai Thong into his dangerous realm. Their duel turns a local river terror into one of Thailand's best-known monster-slayer tales.

name_th: พระอภัยมณี
locality: Rayong coast and Ko Samet imagination trail
dex_note: May accidentally lull an entire beach into nap mode.
origin_story: Phra Aphai Mani is a prince whose chosen weapon is a magical flute, not a sword. Banished after studying music instead of warfare, he wanders through islands, sea kingdoms, and uneasy romances. His song can charm, soothe, or overpower listeners, making him a hero who wins by feeling as much as fighting.

name_th: นางผีเสื้อสมุทร
dex_note: Keep snacks nearby if you hear dramatic waves.
origin_story: Nang Phisua Samut is the powerful sea giantess who falls for Phra Aphai Mani after hearing his flute. She hides her giant form and keeps him in her island cave, hoping love can become a world of its own. When he escapes with their son Sin Samut, her grief and rage rise like a storm across the sea.

lore_age: Likely late 15th to early 16th century Lanna-Ayutthaya romance tradition
dex_note: Has excellent hair and questionable field-trip judgment.
origin_story: Phra Lo is the celebrated prince at the heart of a tragic northern romance. A magical golden peacock draws him toward the twin princesses Phra Phuean and Phra Phaeng, even though their kingdoms carry old hostility. Love pulls the three together, but politics and fate close around them.

name_th: พระเพื่อน–พระแพง
lore_age: Likely late 15th to early 16th century Lanna-Ayutthaya romance tradition
dex_note: They absolutely coordinate outfits before any prophecy.
origin_story: Phra Phuean and Phra Phaeng are twin princesses whose beauty and longing set the Lilit Phra Lo romance in motion. They fall for Phra Lo from afar and help draw him to their city through magic and secret messages. Their love story becomes tender, risky, and finally tragic when family rivalry overtakes desire.

lore_age: 15th-century Pannasa Jataka tradition, later carried through southern Nora performance
dex_note: Leaves a trail of tiny bell sounds and confidence.
origin_story: Manora is a kinnari, a graceful half-bird heroine who is captured after her magical wings are taken. She marries Prince Suthon, but palace jealousy forces her to flee back to her celestial homeland. Her story survives through southern Thai Nora performance, where dance becomes memory, longing, and return.

lore_age: 15th-century Pannasa Jataka tradition, later carried through southern Nora performance
dex_note: Will cross an entire map for one good reunion cutscene.
origin_story: Suthon is the prince who loves Manora after she is brought into the human world. When she is driven away, he refuses to let the story end at separation. He journeys through forests and trials to reach her celestial home, turning the romance into a quest for loyalty and reunion.

name_th: หลวงปู่ทวด
dex_note: Turns stress into fresh water and a deep breath.
origin_story: Luang Pu Thuad is a revered southern monk remembered as both a historical figure and a miracle-working protector. Legends say he showed wisdom from childhood and later helped travelers by turning seawater into fresh water. His story grew into a tradition of calm courage, compassion, and protection on difficult journeys.
